title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen deduces that the killer, Crocecki, intentionally left Thomas Braid's note at the crime scene to facilitate his own investigation. Ellery and Eleanor conclude that by delaying the discovery of the note, Crocecki could search for Andrew Van privately. If Crocecki failed to find Van, the eventual discovery of the note would lead the police to Stephen Megalos, who would then be forced to reveal Van's whereabouts.

Megalos reveals that he, Braid, and Van were actually brothers whose real surname is Tvervel. They had assumed aliases years ago to hide their identity from their own family. Megalos explains that the killer has been pursuing them for years as an act of revenge, using "T" symbols—such as blood-written letters and headless bodies—to humiliate the Tvervel family. Megalos notes that they have not seen Crocecki in twenty years and cannot recognize him, though he mentions that Crocecki previously had a slight limp that may have been feigned. When Ellery questions how Megalos could know Van was still alive despite being out of touch with the world, Megalos responds that the situation was part of a long-term arrangement related to the Arroyo incident.
